description: This pipeline reads in an image, applies a morphological operation to it, and outputs the result. The main steps include applying a morphological operation to the input image and outputting the result.

e44e6504-88e2-4565-a7b1-c2e73802e2a0.name: ij1-roi-statistics
e44e6504-88e2-4565-a7b1-c2e73802e2a0.description:
Generates a results table containing ROI statistics. If a reference image is provided, the statistics are calculated for the reference image. Otherwise, an empty reference image is automatically generated.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

fc2178f1-9e27-4664-9db5-ba339026ccad.name: ij1-threshold-auto2d
fc2178f1-9e27-4664-9db5-ba339026ccad.description:
Applies an auto-thresholding algorithm. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

c4daad47-3490-4b20-81a3-81ce3ebacc6b.name: io-interface
c4daad47-3490-4b20-81a3-81ce3ebacc6b.description: Passes its input to its output without changes.

748cdad3-eb47-4178-be0a-8ed0ac6a2ccf.name: table-merge-tables
748cdad3-eb47-4178-be0a-8ed0ac6a2ccf.description:
Merges multiple tables into one table by merging the list of rows. Columns are automatically created if they do not exist.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of multiple data items. If you want to group all data into one output, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave 'Data set matching annotations' empty.

3e2144f4-ffd2-42a2-bcf9-28627b21b67e.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
3e2144f4-ffd2-42a2-bcf9-28627b21b67e.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

3f5fbaf4-01a4-4d86-b01c-b0ac977dae3a.name: ij1-morph-operation2d
3f5fbaf4-01a4-4d86-b01c-b0ac977dae3a.description:
Applies a morphological operation to greyscale images. Following operations are included: Erosion, Dilation, Opening, Closing, White Top Hat, Black Top Hat, Gradient, Laplacian, Internal Gradient, and External Gradient. More information (including examples) can be found at https://imagej.net/MorphoLibJ.html
If a multi-channel image is provided, the operation is applied to each channel. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

493ea08b-9ad2-4788-a31e-036bfb848181.name: ij1-background-rollingball2d
493ea08b-9ad2-4788-a31e-036bfb848181.description: Finds or subtracts the background of a 2D slice via a rolling-ball or paraboloid algorithm. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

4fd98fe3-07f4-4ed4-aa37-afb53f9529ce.name: ij1-roi-filter-statistics
4fd98fe3-07f4-4ed4-aa37-afb53f9529ce.description:
Filters the ROI list elements via statistics.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

841a2de0-97af-471b-ab90-8484dc9cad31.name: ij1-roi-statistics
841a2de0-97af-471b-ab90-8484dc9cad31.description:
Generates a results table containing ROI statistics. If a reference image is provided, the statistics are calculated for the reference image. Otherwise, an empty reference image is automatically generated.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

561e5bb0-edc2-43d3-8d9c-28ca273b84a5.name: ij1-segment-ridge-detector-2d
561e5bb0-edc2-43d3-8d9c-28ca273b84a5.description: A ridge detector that detects lines and outputs a binary mask where lines are detected.

853c09b1-65bd-4336-af9f-464cd1b86d48.name: ij1-roi-merge
853c09b1-65bd-4336-af9f-464cd1b86d48.description:
Merges multiple ROI lists by using data annotations. By default, ROIs with equivalent annotations are put into the same group and merged into one ROI list for each group. Use the parameters to control how groups are created. To merge all incoming ROI lists into just one list, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave the list of annotation columns empty.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of multiple data items. If you want to group all data into one output, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave 'Data set matching annotations' empty.

547b8e29-00ab-483e-a774-8bdbb79c43aa.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
547b8e29-00ab-483e-a774-8bdbb79c43aa.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

1bc71122-8f75-45cb-b5ba-0cf423cb1875.name: cellpose-2
1bc71122-8f75-45cb-b5ba-0cf423cb1875.description:
Deprecated. Use the node with the same name.

Runs Cellpose on the input image. This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged

4672dfd4-f997-441d-8b7e-13bff8dc7598.name: ij1-math-imagecalculator2d
4672dfd4-f997-441d-8b7e-13bff8dc7598.description:
Applies a mathematical operation between two images. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice. Old implementation that directly uses the one provided by ImageJ. We recommend to use 'Fast image arithmetics' instead.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

eb77a55d-a98b-48e6-8ef6-4f883f78eb1b.name: ij1-roi-filter-statistics
eb77a55d-a98b-48e6-8ef6-4f883f78eb1b.description:
Filters the ROI list elements via statistics.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

7623eb88-6b0d-419e-89fa-532d1ca7fff4.name: ij1-roi-statistics
7623eb88-6b0d-419e-89fa-532d1ca7fff4.description:
Generates a results table containing ROI statistics. If a reference image is provided, the statistics are calculated for the reference image. Otherwise, an empty reference image is automatically generated.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

c198d89a-b4c5-44ef-a067-d4e75a5d2126.name: plot-table:plot-histogram
c198d89a-b4c5-44ef-a067-d4e75a5d2126.description: Plots the incoming table(s). This node will create a JFreeChart Histogram plot.
